$schema_note: Conformance fixture for 'Script Run Extraction from Mixed-Script Text', spec v2.0. This file is the machine-readable companion referenced in spec section 12 and is authoritative alongside the plain-text spec source, as ORIGINAL BYTES parsed once by a conforming JSON parser: copies that have passed through an additional encoding layer (embedded in other JSON, code blocks, logs) re-escape backslashes and are not authoritative. Inputs/expected are exact JSON strings; parse with a conforming JSON parser and compare code points, not glyphs. The 'cases' array reproduces the v1.4 fixture latin-run-extraction-tests.json unchanged and is the Latin backward-compatibility suite (spec section 12.1, run under target_script = Latin); 'generalization_cases' (spec section 12.2) carry an explicit per-case target_script.
notes: All expected outputs assume default_policy unless a policy_sensitivity entry states otherwise. Cases in 'cases' run under target_script = Latin; cases in 'generalization_cases' each carry an explicit target_script that overrides default_policy for that case only.

min_target_letters is the v2.0 name of v1.4's min_latin_letters (spec section 9). Implementations MAY accept min_latin_letters as an alias when target_script = Latin and MUST document if they do. A default_policy lacking a target_script key (as in the v1.4 fixture) means Latin.

policy_sensitivity maps knob -> non-default setting -> expected runs under that setting. These entries are SELECTED normative variants, NOT an exhaustive sensitivity map: the absence of an entry for a knob does NOT imply the case is unaffected by that knob (see policy_sensitivity_is_exhaustive and spec section 12). Broad knobs such as min_target_letters, max_bridge, and affinity_overrides inherently affect many cases.

input_codepoints, where present, is a redundancy check for cases containing invisible or easily-corrupted characters. In generalization_cases it also guards cross-script confusables: Greek Alpha U+0391, Cyrillic A U+0410, and Latin A U+0041 are visually identical in many fonts, and a copy-paste substitution silently changes a case's class structure. Verify code points, never glyphs.

In default_policy, max_bridge: null represents the specification default of infinity, i.e. no bridge-length limit (spec sections 7.1 and 9). Harnesses must not treat null as zero, missing, or invalid.

input_codepoints lists are the ground truth for escaping questions: verify a suspected escape (e.g. \n vs \\n) against the parsed string's code points, never against a re-encoded display of this file.

Generalization case G5 corrects the worked example that circulated with the v2.0 design delta: with target_script = Arabic, the final word of 'עברית © 2026 العربية نهاية' is itself Arabic (TARGET), so the space before it sandwich-merges (spec 7.1) and the run is '© 2026 العربية نهاية' — not '© 2026 العربية'. G4 preserves the delta's intended demonstration (leading © anchor toward an RTL target) by making the trailing word Hebrew.

cases.exercises: sandwich: digits, space, parens
sandwich (internal URL glue) + trailing digit commit through SEP '='
trailing anchor U+2122

cases.input: 텍스트. © 2026 Watch Tower Bible and Tract Society of Pennsylvania
expected: © 2026 Watch Tower Bible and Tract Society of Pennsylvania
cases.exercises: leading anchor commits provisionals; stops at LEFT '.'
provisionals released without anchor
bridging comma excluded both sides
opener as leading anchor, closer as trailing anchor; Hangul-only paren group yields nothing
hard-break wall; leading glue after wall; input contains a real U+000A LINE FEED (single JSON escape \n in source) - the input_codepoints list is the authoritative check against escaping corruption
trailing '+' anchor; bare leading numeral released
dangling neutral, no host — discarded
Arabic-Indic digits are BASE, not provisional glue
European digits provisional, no anchor, released
leading + trailing boundaries vs RTL base
control strip -> re-coalesce -> trailing digit-group commit (spec 7.5 rule 2)
unpaired control shed, never absorbed
U+3002 boundary, basic form. NOTE: this case's output is identical whether U+3002 classifies BASE (spec 5.2e) or neutral-SEP, so it does not discriminate the classification; case 24 does.

trailing digits consumed by ',' LEFT anchor (spec 7.5 rule 1); strip_terminal_punct removes host-sentence comma; NOT sensitive to trailing_digits_bind (comma anchor commits regardless). The strip_terminal_punct=off variant is the REGRESSION TEST for spec 11.2: a LEFT anchor must clear open digit-group state, else a later close_group() moves the committed boundary backward and drops the comma.
trailing digit group abutting EDGE self-commits
abutment exception: digit group contiguous with following BASE released
totality: '-' falls to default SEP, committed transitively by digit group; sensitive to trailing_digits_bind (off: '-123' stays provisional and is released)

emoji at a run boundary is never absorbed (basic form). NOTE: does not by itself discriminate STOP from SEP affinity for the pictographic - both yield 'Party' here; case 25 is the discriminating test.
spaced digit group closes before run end -> self-commits; deliberate contrast with case 19; sensitive to trailing_digits_bind (off: digit group does not self-commit)
cluster-wide STOP test (spec 7.3 step 3): keycap U+0031 U+FE0F U+20E3 is STOP, not DIGIT
U+3002 classified BASE (spec 5.2e); prevents LATIN-NEUTRAL-LATIN sandwich merge; discriminating test for cjk_punct_strong
Extended_Pictographic STOP blocks the trailing scan from reaching a later LEFT anchor (U+2122); a SEP misclassification of the emoji would yield 'Party 🎉™'
spec 10.1 iterative terminal stripping: run-final ')' is not a terminal, so the period is protected; output identical under strip_terminal_punct=off
spec 10.1 malformed-nesting resolution: unmatched retained closer still protects the period (retention is normative); output identical under strip_terminal_punct=off

full-width digits U+FF10-U+FF19: BASE under spec 5.2e strengthening, and never DIGIT affinity (spec 7.3, ASCII-only) - unlike ASCII digits (case 18) they are never captured as a trailing digit group. NOTE: output is identical under cjk_punct_strong=off (NEUTRAL with default SEP affinity, no anchor, released), so this case pins the default but does not discriminate the knob; case 29 does.
discriminating test that full-width DIGITS specifically (not only punctuation) are in the spec 5.2e strengthening set; a NEUTRAL classification sandwich-merges to a single run

generalization_cases.exercises: trailing digit-group self-commit with a non-Latin target (mirror of Latin cases 1/18); Greek letters are U+0391 U+03B8 U+03AE U+03BD U+03B1 - verify against confusable Latin
base rule: Script = Greek -> TARGET; whole-string run; validation passes with min_target_letters = 1
Cyrillic, Latin, and Hangul letters are all OTHER under a Greek target; no target-script host anywhere -> no runs; Cyrillic 'Москва' is U+041C U+043E U+0441 U+043A U+0432 U+0430, not Latin lookalikes

leading © RIGHT anchor commits provisionals toward an RTL target; anchor rules are script-independent (mirror of Latin case 13 with host and target roles swapped: Hebrew host, Arabic target); trailing boundary breaks before the following Hebrew word

sandwich rule with an RTL target: the space between the two Arabic words is TARGET-NEUTRAL-TARGET and merges (spec 7.1). CORRECTION of the v2.0 design-delta example, which gave '© 2026 العربية' for this input by overlooking that نهاية is Arabic; see fixture notes
v1.4 equivalence spot check (spec 2.1): explicit target_script = Latin reproduces the Latin-suite behavior; structural duplicate of case 1 without parens
spec 5.2(d) generalization: Arabic-Indic digits U+0662 U+0660 U+0662 U+0666 are strong OTHER under a Greek target, never provisional glue (mirror of Latin case 11)
spec 10.2 validation: the leading scan finds no TARGET neighbor (no Greek anywhere), the neutral run is OTHER/EDGE-flanked and discarded; nothing survives to validate
spec 10.2 validation: Latin letters do not satisfy a Greek min_target_letters threshold; 'ABC' is OTHER under a Greek target, so no run is ever formed
Cyrillic target: trailing digit-group self-commit (mirror of G1/case 18); 'Москва' code points are Cyrillic, not Latin confusables
Hebrew target inside an Arabic host: trailing ™ LEFT anchor absorbed (mirror of Latin case 3); RTL-target/RTL-host processing is still purely logical-order
